The director has to schedule the scenes with shooting days 4 6 8 10 12 while ensuring no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The release years of the movies are 1994 2004 2007 2012 2015 which belong to the decades {1990-1999} {2000-2009} {2010-2019} respectively. To minimize the total shooting days the optimal sequence is 1994 2004 2012 2007 2015 with total days 4 6 10 8 12 resulting in 40 days. The constraint is satisfied as no two consecutive scenes are from the same decade. The total number of shooting days is minimized.  
******  
The director has a budget of ₹50 lakhs and costs per day for scenes are ₹1 lakh ₹1.5 lakhs ₹2 lakhs ₹2.5 lakhs ₹3 lakhs. The objective is to maximize the number of completed scenes within the budget. The linear programming problem is formulated with variables representing days for each scene and constraints on total cost. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed is 4 with total cost ₹40 lakhs. The optimal solution includes scenes with days 4 6 8 10 resulting in 4 scenes. The maximum number of scenes that can be completed within the budget is 4.
